warnings: Dropped 2 non-scored row(s) worth 0 points (Dress Code Penalty, Late Arrival Penalty). Check they really aren't scored.
_review.note: Machine-parsed from the official rating sheet. plan.md F3: nobody may be graded against this until a human confirms it.
_review.paraphrase_note: Descriptions and level descriptors are restated in our own words (plan.md §20). Criterion names and every point value are exactly as published on the official rating sheet.

criteria.name: Project Concept & Design Evaluation: Fully address the concept/topic
criteria.description: Assesses the extent to which the animated video successfully covers the assigned concept or topic.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: The central idea or subject matter is entirely disregarded.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: The main idea or subject is present but lacks complete elaboration.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Completely covers the assigned concept or subject matter.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Someone unfamiliar with the concept or topic can clearly discern it solely from watching the video.

criteria.name: Project Concept & Design Evaluation: Color, contrast, background, typography, sound, and design are appropriate for the concept/topic
criteria.description: Assesses if the visual and auditory design components are suitable for the given concept or topic.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: Visual and audio components are absent, lack uniformity, or bear no relevance to the main idea or subject.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: A few design selections are fitting, yet problems exist with their uniformity, ease of understanding, or connection to the subject.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The majority of design features, including hues, visual distinction, backdrops, text styles, and audio, are suitably selected and effectively reinforce the concept or topic.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Every design element is expertly chosen and deliberately implemented to improve the concept or topic. Both visual and auditory selections collaborate to form a powerful, unified presentation.

criteria.name: Project Concept & Design Evaluation: Graphics are appropriate for concept/topic
criteria.description: Assesses whether the visual images used are suitable for the concept or topic.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: Visuals are absent, irrelevant to the subject, or do not connect to the main idea.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: A portion of the visuals pertains to the subject, but their intent or standard is vague or does not align with the core concept.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The visuals are mostly fitting and support the concept or topic. They are clear and contribute to the animation's message.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The visuals are expertly crafted, consistently align with the concept, and significantly enhance the narrative. Visual choices are deliberate and add clarity, mood, or emphasis to the topic.

criteria.name: Project Concept & Design Evaluation: Information related to the topic is accurate
criteria.description: Assesses the factual correctness of information presented in the video concerning the topic.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No information related to the topic is provided, or all information given is incorrect.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Some information is pertinent, but there are clear inaccuracies or significant details are missing.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The information is largely accurate and clearly linked to the topic. Any minor errors do not hinder comprehension.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: All information is precise, thoroughly researched, and clearly supports the topic. The content enhances understanding and adds depth to the animation.

criteria.name: Project Evaluation: Overall quality of elements of animation
criteria.description: Assesses the overall standard of the animation's constituent parts.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: The animation lacks fundamental components like movement or proper timing; it appears unfinished or disconnected.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: The animation exhibits limited use of animation principles. Movements might be unpolished, inconsistent, or distracting.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The animation shows a solid application of basic animation elements. Movements, timing, and transitions are generally fluid and support the narrative or message.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The animation is refined and seamless, with a strong, consistent application of animation principles. Timing, movement, and transitions enhance storytelling and deliver a professional-grade experience.

criteria.name: Project Evaluation: Quality of editing and transition
criteria.description: Assesses the standard of editing and transitions within the video.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No transitions or evidence of video editing are included.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Transitions are present but are abrupt and do not connect smoothly.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Transitions flow effortlessly and serve as a sensible part of the video.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Various types of transitions are employed, and all integrate seamlessly.

criteria.name: Project Evaluation: Logical flow of thoughts
criteria.description: Assesses the coherent progression of ideas within the video.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: The video's progression appears illogical.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: The progression fails to link each component of the video.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The progression is sensible and appears to follow a sequence.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The progression is sensible, including a distinct beginning, middle, and end.

criteria.name: Project Evaluation: Video concludes with proper credits and acknowledgement of copyright and sources
criteria.description: Assesses whether proper credits and acknowledgment of copyright and sources are included.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: The video does not contain credits.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: The video includes credits, but they are incomplete.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The video includes credits that identify the creator, contributors, and acknowledge materials used.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The video includes credits that identify the creator, contributors, and features written acknowledgment of copyright and sources.

criteria.name: Video Time
criteria.description: Assesses if the video adheres to the specified maximum duration.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: The video exceeds 2 minutes in length.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The video is 2 minutes or less.

criteria.name: Demonstrates understanding of the event topic
criteria.description: Assesses the competitor's comprehension of the event topic during their presentation.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No understanding of the event topic is shown, or an incorrect topic was used.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Understanding of the topic is inconsistent throughout the presentation.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The topic is clearly conveyed through the presentation.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Expertise in the topic is clearly demonstrated through the presentation.

criteria.name: Describes the development process
criteria.description: Assesses the explanation of how the animation was conceived and planned.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No description of the development process is provided.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: The development process is explained, but no concrete planning documents are shared.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The development process is explained, and tangible planning documents are shared.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The development process is explained using industry-specific terms, and tangible planning documents are displayed.

criteria.name: Describes the production process
criteria.description: Assesses the explanation of how the animation was created and executed.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No description of the production process is provided.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: The production process is explained, but no concrete planning documents are shared.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The production process is explained, and tangible planning documents are shared.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The production process is explained using industry-specific terms, and tangible planning documents are displayed.

criteria.name: Describes software and hardware used
criteria.description: Assesses the explanation of the software and hardware utilized for the animation.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No explanation of the software and hardware used is provided.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Only the software OR the hardware used is explained.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Both the software AND hardware used are explained.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The software and hardware used are explained, along with alternative options and the justification for the final choices.

criteria.name: Describes animation techniques used
criteria.description: Assesses the explanation of the animation methods employed.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No explanation of the animation techniques used is provided.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: The animation techniques used are identified.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The animation techniques used are identified and explained with industry-specific terms.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The animation techniques used are identified and explained with industry-specific terms, and examples are provided.

criteria.name: Copyright and source information is displayed, documented, and video content is original
criteria.description: Assesses the presentation and documentation of copyright and source details, as well as the originality of the video's content.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: Copyright information or source details are not included, or the video content is not original.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: The material infringes upon copyright or source guidelines, or the video content is not original.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Copyright information and sources are documented, and the video content is original.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Copyright compliance is documented, sources are presented using visual representation, and the video content is original.

criteria.name: Statements are well-organized and clearly stated
criteria.description: Assesses the structure and clarity of remarks made during the presentation.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: The competitor(s) did not appear ready.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: The competitor(s) were ready, but the presentation lacked a logical progression.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The presentation followed a logical sequence.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The presentation followed a logical sequence, and all statements were well-structured.

criteria.name: Consistently displays confidence, uses lively language, engaging eye contact, and effective voice projection
criteria.description: Assesses the delivery skills demonstrated during the presentation.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: None of the listed skills were demonstrated.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: One or two of the listed skills (confidence, engaging language, eye contact, or voice projection) were demonstrated.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Three of the listed skills (confidence, engaging language, eye contact, or voice projection) were demonstrated.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: All skills were demonstrated, thereby enhancing the overall presentation.

criteria.name: Demonstrates the ability to effectively answer questions
criteria.description: Assesses the competitor's capability to provide effective answers to questions.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No response to questions, or responses are completely irrelevant.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Provides incomplete or unclear answers that indicate limited understanding.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Responds accurately and concisely to most questions, showing sufficient understanding.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Responds confidently with accurate, complete, and insightful answers that enhance the overall presentation.

criteria.name: Adherence to Competitive Events Guidelines
criteria.description: Assesses compliance with all competitive event guidelines for the execution of the presentation.

levels.label: Competitor(s) Did Not Follow Guidelines
levels.descriptor: The competitor(s) failed to adhere to the guidelines.

levels.label: Execution Aligned with Guidelines
levels.descriptor: The pre-recorded segment did not exceed 2 minutes; only permissible technology devices were used (sizing specifications were met; a maximum of two, with only one facing judges at a time); the presentation aligned with the assigned topic; professional boundaries were maintained during set-up time (no interaction with judges); no materials were left behind after the presentation; links or QR codes were displayed appropriately (not clicked or scanned by judges); audio was presented without external speakers (preliminary round); the use of food or live animals was avoided.
